verified_title: The secretion genes of Pseudomonas aeruginosa alkaline protease are functionally related to those of Erwinia chrysanthemi proteases and Escherichia coli alpha-haemolysin
note: Guzzo 1991 Mol Microbiol: AprDEF (and hlyB/D/tolC) mediate secretion of P. aeruginosa alkaline protease AprA; correct organism+protein, replaces wrong Serratia/Erwinia 1997 ref

verified_title: Secretion of cyclolysin, the calmodulin-sensitive adenylate cyclase-haemolysin bifunctional protein of Bordetella pertussis
note: Glaser 1988 EMBO J: cyaBDE-dependent secretion of adenylate cyclase-haemolysin (CyaA). Note this is B. pertussis, not B. bronchiseptica; foundational CyaBDE secretion ref, no bronchiseptica-specific secretion assay found

verified_title: Genetic and biochemical characterization of PrtA, an RTX-like metalloprotease from Photorhabdus
note: Bowen 2003 Microbiology: PrtA is a secreted RTX metalloprotease; prtA sits with its ABC transporter prtBCD. Correct Photorhabdus PrtA, replaces wrong Serratia/Erwinia 1997 ref

verified_title: Prevalence of local immune response against oral infection in a Drosophila/Pseudomonas infection model
note: Liehl 2006 PLoS Pathog: AprA is an abundant secreted metalloprotease of P. entomophila (aprA mutant attenuated); correct organism+protein, replaces spurious CooB/CS1 pilin DOI. Secretion machinery not directly mutated

verified_title: Sequence analysis and transcription of the apxI operon (hemolysin I) from Actinobacillus pleuropneumoniae
note: Frey 1994 Gene: full apxI operon incl secretion genes apxIB/apxID; better primary than the Frey 1993 nomenclature paper (old_doi). No dedicated ApxI secretion-mutant assay found in PubMed

verified_title: Functional characterization of a cloned haemolysin determinant from E. coli of human origin, encoding information for the secretion of a 107K polypeptide
note: Mackman & Holland 1984 Mol Gen Genet: Tn5 mutants in the export region block translocation of the 107K HlyA across the OM (genuine secretion-mutant assay). Stronger secretion primary than Felmlee 1985 sequence paper

No B. trehalosi-specific Lkt secretion assay exists in PubMed (searched Bibersteinia/Pasteurella trehalosi lktA). Accept family-level RTX/Lkt secretion; keep cited P. haemolytica ref and flag as family-level
No M. glucosida-specific Lkt secretion assay exists in PubMed. Accept family-level RTX/Lkt secretion; keep cited P. haemolytica (Highlander 1990) ref and flag as family-level

verified_title: Protease secretion by Erwinia chrysanthemi: the specific secretion functions are analogous to those of Escherichia coli alpha-haemolysin
note: Letoffe 1990 EMBO J (PrtDEF primary): prtD/E/F mutants abolish secretion of E. chrysanthemi proteases. PrtA secreted via the shared PrtDEF system (assayed directly for PrtB/C, PrtA by the same exporter). Hint-recommended ref

verified_title: Genetic and biochemical characterization of PrtA, an RTX-like metalloprotease from Photorhabdus
note: Bowen 2003 Microbiology: secreted RTX metalloprotease PrtA with ABC transporter prtBCD in Photorhabdus (P. luminescens/laumondii lineage). Correct organism, replaces wrong Serratia/Erwinia 1997 ref

verified_title: Interaction between the autokinase EpsE and EpsL in the cytoplasmic membrane is required for extracellular secretion in Vibrio cholerae
note: Sandkvist 1995 EMBO J: EpsE/EpsL required for translocation of cholera toxin across the OM (direct CT-Eps secretion dependence). Replaces Sikora 2011 secretome that did not detect CtxA/B

verified_title: Interaction between the autokinase EpsE and EpsL in the cytoplasmic membrane is required for extracellular secretion in Vibrio cholerae
note: Sandkvist 1995 EMBO J: Eps-dependent translocation of assembled cholera toxin (A+B) across OM. Same direct CtxAB-Eps primary as R023

Could not locate Ball 2002 Hxc/LapA primary via PubMed search within budget; Filloux 2011 (old_doi) is a review. LapA(PA0688) is the Hxc T2SS substrate; needs manual lookup of Ball et al. 2002 Mol Microbiol Hxc paper

verified_title: The lytic polysaccharide monooxygenase CbpD promotes Pseudomonas aeruginosa virulence in systemic infection
note: Askarian 2021 Nat Commun: CbpD(PA0852) is a secreted LPMO virulence factor (cbpD deletion attenuates); CbpD primary replacing Filloux 2011 review. Xcp-dependence of secretion not directly mutated in this paper

verified_title: Protein secretion in gram-negative bacteria: transport across the outer membrane involves common mechanisms in different bacteria
note: Filloux 1990 EMBO J: foundational xcp paper, xcp mutants block OM translocation of P. aeruginosa exoproteins (system primary). No clean per-protein LasA Xcp secretion-mutant paper surfaced; replaces Filloux 2011 review with the primary xcp ref

verified_title: Protein secretion in gram-negative bacteria: transport across the outer membrane involves common mechanisms in different bacteria
note: Filloux 1990 EMBO J: foundational xcp paper establishing Xcp-dependent OM translocation of exoproteins (elastase LasB is the textbook Xcp substrate). System primary replacing the 2011 review; no standalone LasB secretion-mutant primary found in budget

verified_title: Protein secretion in gram-negative bacteria: transport across the outer membrane involves common mechanisms in different bacteria
note: Filloux 1990 EMBO J xcp primary (Michel 2000 J Bacteriol confirms LipA is Xcp-secreted but is an LPS/Xcp-function study). Replaces 2011 review with system primary

verified_title: Protein secretion in gram-negative bacteria: transport across the outer membrane involves common mechanisms in different bacteria
note: Filloux 1990 EMBO J xcp system primary. No dedicated LipC(PA4813) Xcp secretion-mutant primary found; replaces 2011 review. Consider manual check for a LipC-specific ref
No per-protein PaAP(PA2939) aminopeptidase Xcp secretion-mutant paper located within budget. Filloux 1990 xcp system primary could substitute; flag for manual lookup
Alkaline phosphatase PhoA(PA3296) Xcp-dependent secretion: no per-protein primary located within budget. Filloux 1990 xcp system primary could substitute; flag for manual lookup

verified_title: Protein secretion in gram-negative bacteria: transport across the outer membrane involves common mechanisms in different bacteria
note: Filloux 1990 EMBO J xcp system primary. No dedicated PlcH(PA0844) Xcp secretion-mutant primary surfaced in budget; replaces 2011 review

verified_title: Protein secretion in gram-negative bacteria: transport across the outer membrane involves common mechanisms in different bacteria
note: Filloux 1990 EMBO J xcp system primary. No dedicated PlcN(PA3319) Xcp secretion-mutant primary found; replaces 2011 review
Protease IV PrpL(PA4175) Xcp-dependent secretion: no per-protein primary located within budget (Wilderman 2001 Infect Immun is the likely candidate). Flag for manual lookup

verified_title: A periplasmic intermediate in the extracellular secretion pathway of Pseudomonas aeruginosa exotoxin A
note: Lu, Mizushima & Lory 1993 J Bacteriol: direct ToxA extracellular secretion assay; N-terminal mutants accumulate periplasmically/block OM export. Replaces Filloux 2011 review with a ToxA-specific secretion primary

verified_title: The type II secretion system of Legionella pneumophila elaborates two aminopeptidases, as well as a metalloprotease that contributes to differential infection among protozoan hosts
note: Rossier 2007 AEM: LapA is a Lsp(T2SS)-secreted aminopeptidase (lapA mutant). Direct LapA-specific Lsp primary; replaces broken soil-ecology DOI. (DebRoy 2006 PNAS secretome is the alternative system primary)

verified_title: Legionella pneumophila type II secretome reveals unique exoproteins and a chitinase that promotes bacterial persistence in the lung
note: DebRoy 2006 PNAS: Lsp(T2SS) secretome of L. pneumophila identifies the type-II-dependent exoprotein set incl GDSL lysophospholipase PlaA. Replaces broken soil-ecology DOI; hint-recommended ref

verified_title: Assembly of the Type II Secretion System: Identification of ExeA Residues Critical for Peptidoglycan Binding and Secretin Multimerization
note: Li 2010/2011 (old_doi) is an ExeA-machinery study that uses Exe-dependent lipase secretion as the readout, so it does demonstrate Exe-dependent export of the A. hydrophila lipase. Defensible as the cited primary; no better substrate-focused Exe lipase paper found

No direct B. cenocepacia ZmpA T2SS-dependence paper found (hits are regulatory/transcriptomic: O'Grady 2010/2011). Corbett 2003 (old_doi) infers GSP from homology only. Needs manual lookup of a ZmpA secretion-mutant study

verified_title: Characterization of the pelL gene encoding a novel pectate lyase of Erwinia chrysanthemi 3937
note: Lojkowska 1995 Mol Microbiol: PelL-specific gene paper (E. chrysanthemi/D. dadantii 3937), correct subject replacing the mis-cited PelN paper. PelL is an Out(T2SS) substrate; this paper characterizes the secreted enzyme but does not itself mutate the Out system
